title: Formation de parolas
summary: Build new words from ones you know: suffixes and prefixes.

teach.english: suffix: a person who ___s / believes in ___
teach.note: arte (art) → artista (artist), already known. Same suffix as English -ist.

teach.english: suffix: turns a verb into an abstract noun
teach.note: ager (to act) → action (action). Matches English -tion/-sion almost exactly.
possibile already uses this shape; compare English -able/-ible.

grammar.title: Interlingua is buildable, not just memorizable
grammar.body:
Interlingua's vocabulary was deliberately drawn from the international scientific/Latinate words shared by English, French, Spanish, Italian — which means its word-building suffixes mostly work exactly like the English ones already in your head:
**-ista** (artist, dentist), **-ion** (action, nation), **-abile/-ibile** (possible, terrible), **-mente** (adverbs, already known).
Once you know a root and a suffix, you can often *construct* a word you were never explicitly taught — and be right.

teach.note: in- + possibile — the negating prefix, same as English im-/in-.
dis- + honeste — same pattern as English dis-.

teach.english: It's impossible to find a solution.

canDo: Guess the meaning of an untaught Interlingua word from its parts
